title: QueryEngine class in py_manifest_augmented.py — 6W1H bidirectional call-chain queries
description:
plan_ref: Agent_plans/cosmic-scribbling-tarjan.md
wherein: Data/tabs/map_tab/py_manifest_augmented.py::QueryEngine (new class)
priority: P2

Add QueryEngine class enabling 6W1H queries against the manifest:
- who: which class/function owns this behaviour
- what: what does this function do (verb_category, docstring)
- where: file path, class, line number
- when: last modified timestamp, session context
- why: inferred from docstring + morphological domain
- how: call graph — what it calls, what calls it
- state: current execution status (PASS/FAIL/WARN from probe)
- chain_delta: diff between two call-chain snapshots

Replace flat 12-element hardcoded weight vector with 8×3 matrix (8 FEATURE_NAMES × 3 OUTCOME_NAMES). Enables bidirectional call-chain traversal reusing PrerequisiteChain.

Powers: Os_Toolkit query 'imports' and 'natural' search types. Also feeds query_weights layer (layer 6) in aoe_vector_config.json.

title: Pooled change-test task spawner — version-linked auto-test per enriched_change
description:
plan_ref: Task_Creation_Convergence_&_System_State_2602.md (Part 6)
wherein: Data/interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py::_run_change_test + Data/backup/change_states/
priority: P0 — reactive cycle foundation

IMPLEMENTED (Feb 20): After every enriched_change is written, a daemon thread runs _run_change_test():
1. Syntax check: ast.parse() on after-state sidecar (Event_{N}_after.py)
2. Import chain test: subprocess python3 -c with 8s timeout, no GUI
3. Writes result to change_states/{safe_id}_test_result.json
4. Back-propagates test_status + test_errors + version_ts into enriched_change in version_manifest
5. If FAIL → appends ATTRIBUTION_GAP alert to checklist.json aoe_inbox
6. Attribution layer in planner board shows: ✓/⚠/❗ icon + @version_date suffix per change node
7. FAIL nodes expand to show error text as child nodes

REMAINING WORK:
- Backfill: run _run_change_test against all 47 existing change_state pairs (Event_0001..0047)
- Pool view: group test results by version_ts in settings_tab backup tree (currently shows per-file)
- Version gate: when all test tasks for a version_ts PASS + no ATTRIBUTION_GAPs → mark version 'test_passed'
- Relaunch signal: on next GUI launch, if version has test_passed=True → show green indicator in version selector
- Loop closure: user marks task COMPLETE on relaunch → conformer checks test_passed → auto-confirm version

title: Implement Thunar UCA "Babel Target" integration
description:
Create right-click actions in Thunar file manager for Babel tools

**Overview:**
Right-click file/folder → "Babel Target" → Smart dispatcher routes to appropriate Babel tool

**Components to Build:**

1. **target.sh** - Smart dispatcher script
   - Detects file type (python, shell, config, directory, etc.)
   - Routes to appropriate Babel command
   - Location: `babel_data/inventory/target.sh`

2. **UCA.XML entries** - Thunar custom actions
   - "Babel: Query File" → Os_Toolkit forensic query
   - "Babel: Query Directory" → Os_Toolkit depth 2
   - "Babel: Grep Flight" → grep_flight GUI with target
   - "Babel: Profile Classes" → Python class profiling
   - "Babel: Organize Files" → Filesync organization
   - "Babel Target" → Smart dispatcher (main action)

3. **install_thunar_actions.sh** - Installer
   - Backs up existing uca.xml
   - Appends Babel actions
   - Uses absolute paths to Babel scripts

**File Type Routing:**
- .py files → `Os_Toolkit.py file "$TARGET" --depth 2 -z` (class profiling)
- Directories → `Os_Toolkit.py "$TARGET" --depth 2 -z`
- Other files → `Os_Toolkit.py "$TARGET" -z`
- Grep action → `grep_flight_babel.py --gui --target "$TARGET"`

**UCA Placeholders:**
- %f = Single file/folder path
- %F = Multiple paths
- %n = Filename without path
- %d = Parent directory

**Testing:**
```bash
# Install
chmod +x babel_data/inventory/target.sh
./install_thunar_actions.sh

# Restart Thunar
thunar -q && thunar &

# Right-click test files:
# - Python file → Should show class profiling
# - Directory → Should show depth 2 analysis
# - Config file → Should show forensic data
# - Any file + "Babel: Grep Flight" → GUI launches
```

**Success Criteria:**
- ✅ target.sh routes correctly for all file types
- ✅ All 6 UCA actions install without errors
- ✅ Right-click shows "Babel" submenu
- ✅ Actions launch appropriate Babel tools
- ✅ File/directory context passed correctly

**Dependencies:** Task #21 (grep_flight integration)

**Deliverable:** Working Thunar integration for quick Babel tool access

title: Design self-contained workflow loop architecture
description:
Document complete self-contained loop connecting all Babel systems

**Vision:** User works entirely within grep_flight GUI, all systems sync automatically

**Loop Components:**

1. **Entry Point: grep_flight Taskboard UI**
   - User sees all todos from 3 sources
   - Clicks buttons to manage tasks
   - Spawns Claude sessions
   - Marks files/code with annotations

2. **Action Flow:**
   ```
   grep_flight UI
       ↓ (user creates task)
   plans/todos.json (Os_Toolkit)
       ↓ (sync_all_todos)
   ~/.claude/tasks/ (Claude CLI source)
       ↓ (also syncs to)
   #[Mark:ID] in code files
       ↓ (user spawns Claude)
   Claude CLI session (positioned terminal)
       ↓ (works on task)
   Creates subtasks in ~/.claude/tasks/
       ↓ (session ends, auto-sync)
   Os_Toolkit syncs back to plans/todos.json
       ↓ (grep_flight polls/refreshes)
   Taskboard UI updates with new tasks
       ↓ (suggestive actions monitors)
   Error detection suggests new todos
       ↓ (loop continues...)
   ```

3. **Auto-Sync Triggers:**
   - On grep_flight task button click → immediate sync
   - On Claude session end → auto-sync
   - On file save (if inotify available) → sync marks
   - On grep_flight refresh → poll all sources
   - Periodic background sync (every 5 min)

4. **Error Handling Loop:**
   ```python
   # grep_flight traceback monitors all operations
   if error_detected:
       # 1. Log to traceback
       log_to_traceback(EventCategory.ERROR, operation, context, error=str(e))
       
       # 2. Suggest action
       suggested_action = SuggestiveGrepEngine.suggest_fix(error)
       
       # 3. Show in UI
       show_suggestion_dialog(suggested_action)
       
       # 4. User clicks "Create Todo"
       create_todo_from_error(error, suggested_action)
       
       # 5. Todo syncs everywhere
       sync_all_todos()
       
       # 6. User can work on it immediately
       # Loop continues...
   ```

5. **Session Continuity (Morning's Work):**
   ```python
   # On grep_flight launch:
   def startup():
       # 1. Load auto-memory
       load_memory('~/.claude/projects/.../memory/MEMORY.md')
       
       # 2. Show recent work in taskboard
       recent_todos = get_recent_completed_todos()
       show_notification(f"Welcome back! {len(recent_todos)} tasks completed last session")
       
       # 3. Suggest next actions
       next_tasks = get_pending_todos()
       suggest_next_task(next_tasks[0])
   ```

6. **Mark Faulty Workflow:**
   ```python
   def mark_faulty(item, reason):
       # 1. Add mark to code
       add_code_mark(item.file, f"#[Mark:FAULTY:{reason}]")
       
       # 2. Create debug todo
       create_todo(
           subject=f"Debug: {item.subject}",
           description=f"Marked faulty: {reason}\nOriginal context: {item.context}",
           tags=['faulty', 'debug', 'auto-created']
       )
       
       # 3. Log to Os_Toolkit journal
       log_journal('debug', f"Faulty item: {item.subject} - {reason}")
       
       # 4. Sync everywhere
       sync_all_todos()
       
       # 5. Suggestive action may recommend fix
       suggest_debug_action(item, reason)
   ```

**Architecture Diagram:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         grep_flight GUI (Master Control)        │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌────────────┐ │
│  │  Taskboard  │  │  Babel   │  │  Traceback │ │
│  │     Tab     │  │  Tools   │  │   Console  │ │
│  └─────────────┘  └──────────┘  └────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
           ↓ syncs ↓           ↓ launches ↓
    ┌──────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
    │ plans/       │      │  Claude CLI     │
    │ todos.json   │←─────│  (positioned)   │
    └──────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
           ↓ syncs ↓              ↓ creates ↓
    ┌──────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
    │ ~/.claude/   │──────│  New tasks in   │
    │ tasks/       │      │  ~/.claude/     │
    └──────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
           ↓ syncs ↓
    ┌──────────────┐      ┌─────────────────┐
    │ #[Mark:ID]   │      │  Os_Toolkit     │
    │ in code      │←─────│  Journal/Logs   │
    └──────────────┘      └─────────────────┘
           ↑                      ↓
           └──────────────────────┘
              Suggestive Actions
           (monitors, suggests, creates todos)
```

**Self-Contained = No Manual Steps:**
- ✅ No need to run `./babel sync-todos` manually
- ✅ No need to switch between terminals
- ✅ No need to remember task IDs
- ✅ No need to manually log errors
- ✅ Everything visible in one UI
- ✅ All actions one click away
- ✅ Auto-sync keeps everything in sync

**Deliverable:** SELF_CONTAINED_LOOP_ARCHITECTURE.md with complete flow diagrams

title: Implement grep_flight Taskboard UI
description:
Create visual taskboard in grep_flight GUI for managing Babel projects/plans/todos

**Core Requirements:**
- Display all todos from plans/todos.json
- Show Claude tasks from ~/.claude/tasks/
- Show #[Mark:ID] annotations from code
- Buttons for: Create, Update, Complete, Defer, Mark Faulty
- Real-time sync with Os_Toolkit todo system
- Visual status indicators (pending/in_progress/completed)

**Integration Points:**

1. **Load Todos from 3 Sources:**
   ```python
   def load_all_todos():
       # Source 1: Claude tasks
       claude_tasks = load_claude_tasks('~/.claude/tasks/')
       
       # Source 2: Os_Toolkit todos
       babel_todos = load_json('plans/todos.json')
       
       # Source 3: Code marks
       code_marks = scan_code_marks(cwd)
       
       # Merge & reconcile
       return reconcile_todos(claude_tasks, babel_todos, code_marks)
   ```

2. **Buttons/Actions:**
   - **New Task** → Creates in all 3 sources
   - **Mark Complete** → Updates status everywhere
   - **Mark Faulty** → Adds #[Mark:FAULTY:reason] + creates debug todo
   - **Spawn Claude** → Launch Claude CLI, auto-sync tasks after session
   - **Sync Todos** → Runs sync_all_todos action
   - **Suggest Actions** → Uses SuggestiveGrepEngine to recommend fixes

3. **Error Detection Integration:**
   ```python
   # Suggestive actions monitors traceback
   def monitor_for_errors():
       traceback_events = grep_flight_traceback.get_events()
       
       for event in traceback_events:
           if event.level == 'ERROR':
               # Auto-suggest creating todo
               suggest_create_todo(
                   subject=f"Fix: {event.operation}",
                   description=f"Error: {event.error}\nContext: {event.context}",
                   tags=['auto-detected', 'error']
               )
   ```

4. **Self-Contained Loop:**
   ```
   User clicks button in grep_flight
       ↓
   Creates/updates task in grep_flight UI
       ↓
   Syncs to plans/todos.json (Os_Toolkit)
       ↓
   Syncs to ~/.claude/tasks/ (Claude CLI)
       ↓
   Adds #[Mark:ID] to code (if file-specific)
       ↓
   User spawns Claude CLI from grep_flight
       ↓
   Claude works on task, creates subtasks
       ↓
   Auto-sync on session end
       ↓
   grep_flight UI updates with new tasks
       ↓
   Suggestive actions detects errors → suggests todos
       ↓
   Loop continues...
   ```

5. **UI Layout (New Tab):**
   ```
   [Taskboard Tab]
   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ Filter: [All] [Pending] [In Progress]  │
   │ [+ New Task] [Sync] [Spawn Claude]     │
   ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
   │ ☐ #21 Integrate grep_flight with...    │
   │ ◼ #24 Implement Thunar UCA integration  │
   │ ☑ #19 Security audit completed          │
   │ ☐ #22 Integrate suggestive grep         │
   │ ...                                     │
   ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
   │ Selected: #24                           │
   │ [Complete] [Defer] [Mark Faulty] [Edit]│
   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   ```

**Testing Workflow:**
```bash
# 1. Launch grep_flight with taskboard
python3 grep_flight_babel.py --gui

# 2. Click "Taskboard" tab
# 3. See all todos from 3 sources
# 4. Click "New Task" → Creates task everywhere
# 5. Click "Spawn Claude" → Claude CLI launches
# 6. In Claude: work on task, create subtasks
# 7. Exit Claude
# 8. Auto-sync runs
# 9. grep_flight taskboard refreshes
# 10. See new tasks from Claude session
# 11. Mark something faulty
# 12. Suggestive action suggests fix
```

**Dependencies:**
- Task #21 (onboarder integration) - DONE
- Task #22 (suggestive grep) - needed for error detection
- Morning's auto-memory work - provides session continuity

**Deliverable:** Self-contained task management loop in grep_flight GUI

title: Safe Testables: Undo + Recovery System Testing
description: HIGH VALUE: Undo button works but risky to test on core files. Need edge case tests that prove undo value without risking system.

title: Move py_manifest_augmented.py + diff_runner.py to tabs/map_tab/
Update PatternDatabase to read from /DeBug logs as real training data
description: Currently PatternDatabase learns from its own execution patterns in isolation. The /DeBug/ logs contain REAL learned data: probe PASS/FAIL records, UX events, diff attributions. PatternDatabase.load() should also scan recent debug logs for SUCCESSFUL_CALL and FAILED_ATTRIBUTE patterns that already exist there.

title: Update todos.json phase_5_9 — UNBLOCKED by pymanifest discovery
description: Phase 5.9 was BLOCKED_ON_DISCOVERY waiting for CLI biosphere. py_manifest_augmented.py IS that system (ManifestEngine + RuntimeTracer + DiffEngine). StaticAnalyzer.analyze() is the 'distillation' function that feeds manifest['profiles'][file_path].

title: Design Debug Profile schema for training_tab
description: Add 'debug_profile' as a profile type in profiles_panel.py. Profile fields: source='pymanifest', weights_path (→ backup/history/pymanifest_weights.json), probe_results_log, pattern_count, last_trained

title: Add weight save/load to PyManifest PatternDatabase
description: PatternDatabase.save() already writes to JSON. Add PatternDatabase.save_as_weights(path) that exports patterns in a format compatible with models_tab weight format. Also add PatternDatabase.load_weights(path) for loading back. Weights = pattern_id → {type, outcome, occurrence_count, context_hash, metadata}.

title: Runner panel: add Debug Profile evaluation option
description: In runner_panel.py, add 'Debug Profile Eval' checkbox alongside Auto-run Baseline. When checked, post-training run includes pymanifest StaticAnalyzer on trained file + write results to PatternDatabase. This creates: train → evaluate → probe → patterns → weights cycle.

title: Models tab: show Debug Profile weights alongside model weights
description: In models_tab.py, when a model is selected, check if backup/history/pymanifest_weights.json exists for that session. If yes, show 'Debug Profile: N patterns, last updated [timestamp]' in the model info panel. Link to undo_changes debug_config tab for full view.

title: Add editable risk threshold config to Debug Config tab
description: In undo_changes.py _create_debug_config_tab(), add Section E: Risk Config with editable fields: min_confidence_for_high (default 0.7), probe_fail_threshold (missing methods count that triggers FAIL), domain_override (dropdown). Save to version_manifest['debug_config'] on Apply.

title: Connect attribution config to live probe thresholds in logger_util
description: probe_tab_execution() in logger_util.py currently uses hardcoded thresholds. Load overrides from version_manifest['debug_config'] if present. This allows per-project tuning of what constitutes PASS/WARN/FAIL.

title: PyManifest DiffEngine → enriched_changes enrichment bridge
description: DiffEngine.compare() produces static vs runtime gaps (methods never called, imports never used, control flow never entered). Feed this into enriched_changes risk_reasons. This is the 'dead code risk' signal that probe_tab_execution() cannot detect (it only checks hasattr, not runtime call coverage).

title: Fix biosphere rendering layout bugs first (#37)
description: Canvas is in wrong panel (setup_left_panel instead of setup_visualization_panel), double-packed, bottom panel pack order wrong. Must fix before adding new view modes.

title: Add codebase view_mode to DigitalBiosphereVisualizer
description: New radio button 'Codebase Map'. On select: run StaticAnalyzer(file_path, src) on all .py files in /Trainer/Data/, collect ClassInfo + ImportInfo, build Node3D for each class (layer=ENTITIES, color=verb_category color map, size=complexity), Connection3D for each import edge. Render with existing scatter/line plot system.

title: Zoom stack + navigation bar in Brain3DVisualization
description: Add self._zoom_stack = [] (list of dicts), self._current_zoom_level = 0. Add back() method that pops stack and reloads. Add breadcrumb label widget above the canvas. Wire Esc key to back(). The zoom_to_level(level, key, data) method swaps node/edge data and re-renders without rebuilding the Figure.

title: Data loader per zoom level
description: New method load_zoom_data(level, key) that calls StaticAnalyzer/PyManifest to build node/edge dicts for the given level and key. Returns {nodes: [...], edges: [...], title: str}. This is the only new logic — renderer stays the same.

title: Live pulse signal from UX_EVENT_LOG into zoom levels 1+2
description: At level_1 (files) and level_2 (classes), check UX_EVENT_LOG for recent events (last 60s). Files/classes with recent activity get animated pulse effect (increase alpha temporarily, fade back). Reuse existing _animate_region_colors() pattern. Off by default — toggle in control panel.

title: Call graph AoE view at level_4 wired to undo_changes
description: At level_4, clicking a callee node opens UndoChangesDialog for that node's file pre-navigated to Blame tab. This closes the loop: navigate fractal → see what a method calls → click into change history → undo if needed. The AoE (Area of Effect) pooling from Task #10 feeds here.

title: Biosphere 3D Visualization mode selector: Hardware | Codebase | Fractal
description: Add 'Fractal' to the biosphere view mode radio buttons. Fractal mode launches the Brain3DVisualization embedded in the viz_frame instead of the matplotlib scatter plot. The biosphere scatter view and brain fractal view are togglable — same tab, different renderer mounted/unmounted.

title: Replace hardcoded _get_component_file_mapping() with dynamic StaticAnalyzer scan
description: brain_viz_3d.py lines 338-384 has 22 hardcoded Ag Forge file paths that don't exist in this project. Replace with _build_dynamic_component_map() that: (1) scans DATA_DIR/**/*.py via StaticAnalyzer, (2) computes dominant verb_category per file, (3) maps verb→lobe via verb_to_lobe_map, (4) reads TAB_REGISTRY source_file entries for registered tabs, (5) writes result to user_prefs/component_map_cache.json (cache, 60s TTL), (6) assigns PyTorch model dir as model_kernel center node. Background thread init, _inject_dynamic_nodes() on main thread via self.after(0,...). Backward compat: keep old static dict as fallback if StaticAnalyzer unavailable.

title: 3D Config System: plot_config.json + brain_map_models/ preset system
description: Introduce user_prefs/plot_config.json (geometry_mode, node_spacing, dynamic_nodes_enabled, cache_ttl, active_preset). Introduce user_prefs/brain_map_models/*.json for node-set presets: trainer_project.json (auto-generated from dynamic scan), blank_canvas.json (empty graph), future tetrahedron_demo.json. Add Canvas Config UI section to brain_viz_3d.py control panel: Mode selector (Trainer | Blank Canvas | Custom), Load Preset combobox, Save As button, Rescan button. Blank Canvas mode zeroes all component lists, shows Add Node button.

title: Integrate TetrahedronExplorer geometry as brain_viz_3d geometry preset
description: The full prototype is in plans/Plans/Project_Digital_Kingdom_001/Tesseracts__and_Tetrahedron.md: generate_64_tetra_grid() generates the 64 tetrahedra from cuboctahedron vertices, tetra_to_json/tetra_from_json for serialization, full matplotlib 3D render with color/opacity/linewidth controls. Integration: extract generate_64_tetra_grid() to a module or inline in brain_viz_3d.py. When geometry_mode='tetrahedron' selected: compute 8 lobe centers from 8 cube_corners of the IVM, assign each of the 8 brain regions to one corner. Node positions within each lobe use barycentric coords inside the tetrahedron instead of random ellipsoid. config.json from prototype maps to user_prefs/plot_config.json.

title: Tesseract (4D hypercube) projection mode for fractal zoom depth visualization
description: 16 vertices (all (±1,±1,±1,±1) combinations), 32 edges projected into 3D via perspective division: screen_x = x/(w+d) where d=2.0. W=1 outer cube = lobe level, W=0 inner cube = file level, W=-1 = class level. Rotating 4D camera for screensaver (rotate in XW or ZW plane). Context as W-coordinate: same 3D position, different W = different zoom depth context. The tesseract IS the fractal zoom levels projected simultaneously. Color by W-layer.

title: Spatiotemporal Acoustic Manifold — AoE wave propagation as 3D surface
description: Create tabs/map_tab/acoustic_manifold.py: a matplotlib 3D surface plot (ax.plot_surface) where X/Y=module layout positions (from _build_dynamic_component_map lobe centers), Z=cumulative event intensity over a rolling time window, color mapped by recent change density. Each time a UX_EVENT_LOG entry fires for a file, a Gaussian 'ripple' is added at that file's XY position and propagates outward with time. Rendered as a height-map surface. This IS the AoE pooling visualization (task #10) expressed as acoustic wave interference. Embed as a mode in the biosphere visualizer or as a new geometry mode in brain_viz_3d.

title: Custom Code Tab: PyManifest Analyze sub-tab + Brain Map node-click bridge
description: Add _create_analyze_tab() to CustomCodeTab as a 6th sub-tab '🔍 Analyze'. File browse → StaticAnalyzer(path, src).analyze() in background thread → show class/method treeview (left pane) + verb_category distribution + detail text (right pane). Brain map node info popup gets 'Open in Custom Code' button that calls self.tab_instances['custom_code_tab']['instance'].open_file_in_analyze(file_path) via the existing tab_instances cross-reference.

title: Brain Map as standalone top-level Planning Tab
description: Add 'planning_tab' to tab_definitions in interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py (alongside settings, training, models, custom_code, map). This tab hosts Brain3DVisualization as a separate instance from the Biosphere sub-tab. Loaded with dynamic node scan on init. Overlay: todos.json phase status shown as node pulse intensity (READY phases = bright, DESIGN = dim, PENDING = off). Blocked on task_11_6 (dynamic nodes must work first).

title: Tool Script Selector UI in Custom Code Tab Tools sub-tab
description: In the existing 🔧 Tools sub-tab, add: (1) A combobox/listbox showing all .json files in tool_schemas_configs/ directory — auto-discovered via os.scandir, (2) File path display showing resolved absolute path with exists() validation indicator (✓ green / ✗ red), (3) 'Load' button that reads the selected JSON and populates tool list below, (4) 'Browse...' button for custom tool script path, (5) Currently active config shown in header. All path operations logged via logger_util.log_ux_event(tab='custom_code_tab', event_type='tool_config_load', widget='tool_selector', outcome='fired'|'error', detail=resolved_path).

title: Call path debug panel for tool invocations
description: Add a collapsible 'Debug' panel below the tool selector showing: (1) Resolved tool script path with each path segment validated, (2) Last tool invocation call chain (tool_name → script_path → args → outcome), (3) Stderr/stdout last output (truncated to last 100 lines), (4) Error classification: PATH_NOT_FOUND | PERMISSION_DENIED | SCHEMA_INVALID | TIMEOUT | SUCCESS. Panel is populated by hooking into whatever calls the tool — intercept at the tool dispatch layer and push to logger_util.log_ux_event() + update debug panel via self.after(0, ...). Critical area: every tool invocation must be traceable.

title: Tool invocation events surfaced in logger_util debug tab + undo_changes
description: Every tool invocation in custom_code_tab gets: (1) log_ux_event() with outcome and detail (maps to UX_EVENT_LOG), (2) If tool script path doesn't exist: log at ERROR level, surface in Debug Config tab of undo_changes.py as a UX Baseline warning, (3) Tool calls routed through safe_command() in base_tab.py so tkinter silent failures are caught. Connects puppeteer tool config back to the existing debug/blame/attribution architecture.

title: Agent.json schema + agent_profiles/ directory + 2 default profiles
description: Create tabs/custom_code_tab/agent_profiles/ directory. Write Main_GUI_Debugger.agent.json (tier:Adept, tools:read+grep+bash, temp:0.2, schema:dev-full.json) and Planner.agent.json (tier:Expert, tools:read+grep+write+git, temp:0.4, schema:search-and-analyze.json). Schema fields: agent_name, version, tier, variant, system_prompt_file, tool_schema, temperature, max_tokens, permissions[], grading_tier, tool_proficiency{}, session_count, avg_grade, corrective_recommendations[], lineage{base_model, variant_path, adapter_used}.

title: Agents sub-tab in custom_code_tab (replaces empty Projects tab)
description: Add '🤖 Agents' sub-tab to custom_code_tab.py replacing the placeholder Projects tab. Left pane: agent list with tier badges. Right pane: config form (name, tier dropdown, variant combobox, system prompt path, tool schema selector, temperature, max_tokens, permissions checkboxes, grading display, tool_proficiency bar). Buttons: New Agent, Import .agent.json, Export, Launch This Agent. Launch action: writes active_agent to mode_settings.json, switches Chat tab to use agent's system prompt + tool schema + temperature. Chat tab header shows agent name + tier badge.

title: Models Tab: rename Levels → Variants, update lineage terminology
description: In tabs/models_tab/models_tab.py: rename 'Levels' sub-tab to '🧬 Variants'. Update _export_base_to_gguf_dialog() and _levels_export_to_gguf() docstrings/UI labels to say 'Variant' not 'Level'. Add variant_id field (format: model_name:quantization) to LineageTracker records. Add 'Variant lineage' section to model Overview tab showing parent PyTorch path → LoRA adapter → this Variant. No logic changes — terminology and display only.

title: Grading schema: GrandMaster→Novice tier computation from ToolCallLogger
description: In agent profile logic: compute tool_proficiency[tool_name] = success_count/call_count from ToolCallLogger JSONL files. Compute overall_grade from: (verified_skill_pct * 0.6) + (avg_tool_proficiency * 0.4). Map to tiers: GrandMaster≥95%, Master≥85%, Expert≥70%, Adept≥55%, Novice<55%. Store in agent.json avg_grade and grading_tier. Add a GradeComputer class in tabs/custom_code_tab/ that reads ToolCallLogger JSONL and returns per-agent stats.

title: Session grading UI: thumbs/grade widget in chat → signal → Training Profile
description: Below each assistant response in chat_interface_tab: add thumbs up/down + tier selector widget (Novice/Adept/Expert/Master/GrandMaster). On grade: store in agent.json session_history[]. If avg_grade drops below tier threshold → set corrective_recommendations[] field with suggested training action. Training Profile tab reads corrective_recommendations to auto-populate next LoRA script suggestion. Connects agent grading back to training loop.

title: Routing Map panel in Tools sub-tab — pipeline diagram + advanced_settings toggles
description: Add collapsible 'Tool Routing Map' section below existing tool list in tools_tab.py. Shows pipeline: [Chat Input] → [IntelligentRouter] → [SchemaValidator] → [ToolOrchestrator] → [ToolExecutor] → [VerificationEngine] → [Chat Output]. Each system gets: toggle button (reads/writes advanced_settings.json), path display with ✓/✗ validation. Systems: IntelligentRouter, SchemaValidator, ToolOrchestrator, FormatTranslator, JSONFixer, VerificationEngine, QualityAssurance, ContextScorer, PreRAGOptimizer. Toggles are direct read/write to advanced_settings.json — already the source of truth.

title: Last tool call debug panel — hook handle_tool_calls() → _tool_debug_log display
description: In chat_interface_tab.py: after each tool call in handle_tool_calls(), append to a module-level _tool_debug_log list (max 20 entries): {tool_name, status, duration_ms, input_preview, output_preview, call_chain_str}. call_chain_str shows which advanced systems were active (check advanced_settings flags). In tools_tab.py: add 'Last Tool Call' panel below routing map. Poll via self.after(2000, _refresh_debug_log). Display: tool name, status, time, input/output preview (64 chars), call chain.

title: Path validation for all routing systems — site-packages ✓/✗ indicators
description: For each advanced system in advanced_settings.json that has a corresponding .py file in site-packages/opencode/: validate file exists on disk. Show ✓ (green) if found, ✗ (red) if missing. Embedded systems (JSONFixer is in chat_interface_tab.py directly) shown with special indicator. Validation runs on panel open and on manual refresh button. Results cached 30s.

title: System Blueprints live call graph — networkx DiGraph from advanced_settings state
description: In settings_tab.py create_blueprints_tab(): add radio selector 'Documentation' / 'Live Call Graph'. Live Call Graph mode: build nx.DiGraph from hardcoded pipeline topology. Node colors from advanced_settings.json: green=enabled, grey=disabled, red=path missing. Edge alpha based on both endpoints enabled. Render via FigureCanvasTkAgg same pattern as brain_viz_3d.py. networkx already imported in digital_biosphere.py — reuse pattern.

title: Pipeline break warnings — orange edges for disabled prerequisites, red nodes for missing paths
description: In the live call graph: if a required predecessor system is disabled but its successor is enabled → orange edge = WARNING. If a system's .py path doesn't exist on disk → red node = BROKEN. Add a warning panel below graph listing all active warnings as text. Refresh every 5s or on advanced_settings.json change.

title: Node-click navigation in System Blueprints — click node → open config panel
description: Bind matplotlib pick events on System Blueprints graph nodes. Clicking a node opens the corresponding config: IntelligentRouter → opens advanced_settings_tab.py to that key, ToolExecutor → opens tools_tab.py, etc. Implement via a node→tab routing dict and parent tab controller navigate_to() call.

title: UnifiedTodoSync: three-way reconciliation between Claude tasks, todos.json, and #[Mark:] annotations
description: Port Babel's UnifiedTodoSync class to Trainer. Reads: ~/.claude/tasks/{session_id}/*.json (Claude tasks), plans/todos.json (project todos), all #[Mark:ID] annotations in /Data/**/*.py. Maps status: pending=open=PENDING, in_progress=in-progress=IN_PROGRESS, completed=done=COMPLETE. Primary key: todos.json task IDs. Claude tasks matched by title similarity (first 50 chars). Mark IDs matched exact. Priority for authoritative status: Claude > todos.json > Mark. Backup todos.json before any write. Output: unified JSON + conflict list. Place at plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py (file already exists there).

title: Action Panel todo sync integration in settings_tab — show P1 items as highlighted actions
description: In settings_tab.py action panel: add a 'Todo Sync' button that runs UnifiedTodoSync.reconcile() and shows conflicts + summary. On refresh: read checklist.json P1 items and highlight them in the Changes treeview with a distinct color (gold). Clicking a highlighted P1 item opens the relevant tab. Source: checklist.json immediate_ready items. Integration note is already in checklist.json _meta.

title: ActivityDetector: file-change to todo correlation for auto-mark suggestions
description: Port Babel's ActivityDetector + MarkSuggestionEngine: detect recently modified files, match to open todos by file references in todo description, compute confidence score, suggest marking complete via dialog. ConformerGate: show Zenity-style tkinter dialog listing suggested actions completed/missing. On confirm: update #[Mark:ID] in code + todos.json status. Trigger: on version_manifest.json change (piggybacking existing poll_changes_list() cycle).

title: Audit Ag_Forge_v1c module structure and identify GUI entry point
description: Read tabs/Ag_Forge_v1c/launch_ag_forge.py (full), Plan_Ag_Forge_v1.md, Onboarding_Integration_Plan.md, and modules/ directory listing. Identify: (1) Which module provides the main tkinter Frame/widget, (2) What dependencies are needed, (3) What data paths it expects (knowledge_forge_data/, logs/), (4) How the AI orchestrator subprocess is managed. Produce integration_spec.md in plans/.

title: Add Ag_Forge tab to interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py
description: Following the modular tab pattern: add AgForgeTab to tab_definitions. AG_FORGE_AVAILABLE flag + lazy import. sys.path fix at import time. Lazy-init on first selection. Per-sub-tab {warn} probe. Tab order gap fix to ensure tab visible even with saved tab_order not containing ag_forge_tab.

title: Wire Ag_Forge debug output to logger_util and Backups & Logs live log
description: Ag_Forge init events and sub-tab probe results are already wired to logger_util via AG_FORGE_PROBE and AG_FORGE_INIT log lines. Next: expose Ag_Forge log file in log combobox in settings_tab.py so master_launch.log is selectable in live log.

title: BUG-001: Fix recovery_util import missing in interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py:607
description: From Plans/Project_Action_Panel_Intergration/latest_bugs.md: 'MAIN_GUI: Error storing tab init results: name recovery_util is not defined'. Line 607 of interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py uses recovery_util but it's not imported at that scope. Fix: add 'import recovery_util' near top of interactive_trainer_gui_NEW.py or at the function scope where it's used. Verify: next launch log should NOT show 'name recovery_util is not defined'.

title: BUG-002: Fix JSON corruption in version_manifest.json — large before/after content causes unterminated string
description: From Plans/Project_Action_Panel_Intergration/latest_bugs.md: 'json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Unterminated string starting at line 2404 column 31 (char 500450)'. Root cause: large before/after file content stored raw in change_states — embedded quotes or newlines not escaped. Fix options: (1) json.dumps() the content strings before embedding, or (2) truncate to first 5000 chars with a '...[truncated]' marker, or (3) store change_states as separate JSON files (change_states/{event_id}.json) and store only the path in version_manifest. Option 3 recommended — avoids manifest bloat and corruption. Verify: refresh_changes_list() must not log JSONDecodeError after next launch.

title: BUG-003: Class context showing N/A in undo popup — nested class property parsing
description: From Plans/Project_Action_Panel_Intergration/latest_bugs.md: 'Class fields showing N/A despite extraction working — need parent class + nested property parsing for AoE'. In logger_util.py log_change_attribution(): the 'classes' extraction from diff text is not reaching nested class properties (self.foo = ... inside __init__). Fix: use StaticAnalyzer._inject_instance_attributes() pattern to extract class-level attributes from the diff context. Surface in undo popup Specs tab. This is related to task_25 (parse nested class properties for AoE attribution).

title: TEST: Verify {wherein} appears in next launch live log
description: After adding wherein=None to log_ux_event() (done in this session), verify the field appears in the live log on next launch. Test steps: (1) Launch app, (2) Open Backups & Logs tab, (3) Click a few buttons/widgets that call log_ux_event(), (4) Check live log for lines containing '∈' (the wherein separator). Also verify wherein column appears in Changes treeview for events that have class context. Document first successful {wherein} log line as proof-of-concept.

title: TEST: Right-click undo test cycle with safe file (task_26 completion)
description: From session notes and task_26 (in_progress): right-click on tab header → Undo Changes → UndoChangesDialog must open for correct event. Test with a non-critical file (temp_test_file.py or similar). Steps: (1) Make a small change to temp_test_file.py, (2) Right-click tab → Undo Changes, (3) Verify dialog opens with correct before/after, (4) Confirm undo restores file, (5) Verify live log shows UNDO event. Mark task_26 complete on success.

title: TEST: Wherein column in Changes treeview shows class context for existing events
description: Verify the wherein column in Changes treeview (added this session) shows meaningful data for existing enriched_changes. Expected: events that have a 'classes' field in their enriched_changes dict should show '∈ ClassName:line' in the Wherein column. Events without class data show empty or line number fallback. Check version_manifest.json enriched_changes for a recent event to confirm 'classes' field is present.

title: GrepFlightPanel(ttk.Frame) adapter — swap tk.Tk, wire to logger_util
description: Create tabs/debug_tab/grep_flight_adapter.py: GrepFlightPanel(ttk.Frame) subclassing ttk.Frame instead of BottomPanel(tk.Tk). Keep GrepSurgicalEngine intact. Replace: log_debug() → logger_util.log_message(). Replace: IPC FIFO path → DATA_DIR/DeBug/grep_flight_ipc.fifo. Replace: VersionManager → recovery_util shim. Shared log monitor watches Trainer debug_log_*.txt instead of unified_traceback.log. Chat tab disabled (provider handled separately). Tabs: 🔍 Grep, 📋 Tasks, 📦 Inventory, 🗂 File Tree.

title: Wire EventCategory.WARNING → {warn} → auto-create INTEGRATE task
description: In grep_flight_adapter.py warning handler + in logger_util.py: when EventCategory.WARNING fires (or when _probe_imports() emits a {warn} line), call auto_create_bug_todo(task_type='INTEGRATE', title=f'Fix import: {module}', wherein=wherein, phase='phase_runtime_bugs'). Writes new INTEGRATE task to todos.json phase_runtime_bugs AND syncs to ~/.claude/tasks/ via TodoSync.

title: Sync Marks action without zenity — pipe to logger_util
description: Replace grep_flight _run_babel_action('sync_marks') zenity pipe with: subprocess.run(['python3', OS_TOOLKIT_PATH, 'actions', '--run', 'sync_marks'], capture_output=True) → logger_util.log_message(stdout). Surface result in Latest Report panel (task_25_2). No zenity dependency in Trainer integration.

title: Port TodoSync from Os_Toolkit → debug_todo_analyzer.py
description: Os_Toolkit already has complete TodoSync / PlanManager / scan_code_marks() implementation. Port to plans/debug_todo_analyzer.py replacing Os_Toolkit path constants with Trainer DATA_DIR. Key methods: PlanManager.scan_code_marks() → scan #[Mark:ID-STATUS] in .py files; TodoSync.sync() → three-way ~/.claude/tasks/ ↔ plans/todos.json ↔ marks; TodoSync._find_latest_claude_session() → reads Claude tasks. This IS task_23_3 — just port from the already-written source.

title: Os_Toolkit latest → Latest Report right panel tab in planner
description: Add '📊 Latest Report' tab to planner_tab.py right_notebook (4th tab, after Ag Knowledge). ScrolledText (readonly) + [🔄 Refresh] + [📋 Copy] buttons. _refresh_latest_report(): subprocess.run(['python3', OS_TOOLKIT_PATH, 'latest'], capture_output=True, timeout=15) → stdout → display widget. Also called on tab show event. OS_TOOLKIT_PATH resolved from Babel_v01a root (constant at module top).

title: Wire Generate Report button → Os_Toolkit latest + Latest Report panel
description: Extend generate_report() in planner_tab.py: 1) save local Refs/report_*.txt as before, 2) run Os_Toolkit.py latest → append output to report file, 3) push combined output to Latest Report right panel tab, 4) logger_util.log_message('[Generate Report] N lines'), 5) auto-switch right_notebook to Latest Report tab.

title: Todo State right panel tab — P0/P1 treeview + Sync Todos button
description: Add '✅ Todo State' tab to planner right_notebook (5th tab). Treeview columns: ID / Title / Status / Priority. Reads plans/todos.json → filters P0/P1 items → color codes by status (READY=green, IN_PROGRESS=yellow, DESIGN=grey, BLOCKED=red). [🔄 Refresh] + [📤 Sync Todos] (calls debug_todo_analyzer sync) + [🔍 Scan Marks] (calls scan_code_marks). Phase summary section below treeview.

title: Provider unification — all chat surfaces → providers.py
description: grep_flight's ProviderManager replaced by thin shim pointing at Ag_Forge modules/quick_clip/providers.py. In grep_flight_adapter.py: from modules.quick_clip.providers import get_provider (replaces ProviderManager). IDE tab (when located) also routes through same providers.py. One provider config, one API key location, one model selection for all chat surfaces.

title: Add INTEGRATE/EXTERNAL/CONFORM/REPORT task types to todos.json schema
description: Update todos.json _meta section with type taxonomy. Add 'type' field to all phase_20+ tasks. INTEGRATE = wiring existing external system into pipeline (conformer validates via import probe + init event + first log_ux_event). EXTERNAL = reference to unported system (manual review only). CONFORM = end-to-end validation task. REPORT = surface state information. Update checklist.json _meta legend.

title: auto_create_bug_todo() in logger_util — {warn} → INTEGRATE task
description: Add auto_create_bug_todo(error_msg, wherein, task_type='INTEGRATE') to logger_util.py. When a {warn} line is emitted (detected by string matching in log_message()), append a new task entry to plans/todos.json → phase_runtime_bugs with: title, wherein, timestamp, type=INTEGRATE or BUG depending on whether it's an import failure or runtime exception. Called from: _probe_imports() failures + base_tab safe_create() exceptions.

title: check_integrate_conformers() — init signals → auto-mark INTEGRATE tasks DONE
